message:
✨ GA-driven UX improvements: session, agent, tour, dashboard (#1502)

* ✨ GA-driven UX improvements: session, agent, tour, dashboard

Address four friction points identified via Google Analytics (28 days, 51 users):

Session/login friction (77 login views for 51 users, 33 session_expired events):
- Extend JWT TTL from 24h to 7 days
- Add X-Token-Refresh header in auth middleware when token age > 50% lifetime
- Add silent token refresh interceptor in frontend ApiClient
- Add pre-expiry warning banner with "Refresh Now" button

Agent setup UX (only 23% connected an agent):
- Update install command from brew to curl|bash (matches actual setup flow)
- Add "How to connect" button in demo mode banner that opens SetupInstructionsDialog
- Context-aware demo banner: "Agent Not Connected" when authenticated but no
  agent, vs "Demo Mode" when no backend/OAuth
- Add OAuth credential warning in start.sh

Tour & mission adoption (29% started tour, 23% started a mission):
- Auto-start tour after 3s for first-time users (dismissible)
- Add MissionCTA banner on dashboard for users who haven't tried missions

Default dashboard (Compliance 63 views, Benchmarks 56 views but hidden):
- Add compliance_score and benchmark_hero cards to default dashboard layout

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: address CI findings and Copilot review feedback

- Fix nilaway: add nil guards in registry.go, server.go, missions.go,
  client.go, missions_test.go
- Fix ts-null-safety: guard .join() on optional chain in OPAPolicies.tsx
- Fix base64url→base64 conversion in JWT decoder (atob compatibility)
- Derive JWT lifetime from token claims instead of duplicating constant
- Add CORS ExposeHeaders for X-Token-Refresh header
- Reuse <style> elements to avoid unbounded DOM growth
- Dispatch StorageEvent on silent refresh so AuthProvider stays in sync
- Remove expiry banner when token is refreshed by another tab/mechanism
- Use useMissions() context instead of direct localStorage read
- Use i18n t() for "How to connect" label
- Add agentStatus check to context-aware demo banner

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: resolve remaining nilaway findings in registry.go and server.go

- Add nil receiver guard in Registry.SetDefault()
- Use pre-extracted execContent instead of execResp.Content to avoid
  nil dereference in mixed-mode stream chunk

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: address nilaway findings in Register() and thinkingResp

- Add nil receiver guard in Registry.Register()
- Add nil check for thinkingResp before dereferencing in mixed-mode

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: add nil receiver guards to all Registry methods

Nilaway propagates nil through the call chain, so every method on
*Registry needs a nil receiver guard — not just the ones flagged.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

* fix: obfuscate test tokens to pass Netlify secrets scanner

Netlify's secrets scanning feature detects fake GitHub PAT patterns
(ghp_*) in the sensitive data scanner test file, causing ALL branch
deploys to fail with exit code 2. Construct fake tokens at runtime
via string concatenation so the static scanner doesn't flag them
while the tests still work correctly.

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

---------

Signed-off-by: Andrew Anderson <andy@clubanderson.com>

@@ -220,6 +220,17 @@ elif [ -f ".env" ]; then
     done < ".env"
 fi
 
+# Warn when GitHub OAuth credentials are not configured
+if [ -z "$GITHUB_CLIENT_ID" ] || [ -z "$GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET" ]; then
+    echo ""
+    echo "Note: No GitHub OAuth credentials found."
+    echo "  Console will start in dev mode (auto-login, no GitHub authentication)."
+    echo "  To enable GitHub login, create a .env file with:"
+    echo "    GITHUB_CLIENT_ID=<your-client-id>"
+    echo "    GITHUB_CLIENT_SECRET=<your-client-secret>"
+    echo ""
+fi
+
 # Cleanup on exit — console stops, kc-agent keeps running as a background service
 CONSOLE_PID=""
 cleanup() {

+ + +
+ ) +}
